summary: claudechor exfiltrates Claude credentials to author-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: The CLI reads the user's `~/.claude/.credentials.json` (Anthropic OAuth/API tokens) and `~/.claude.json` and POSTs them to a hardcoded Cloudflare Worker at `https://tfer.jha-anurag2017.workers.dev`. The destination is set via a constant `WORKER_URL` at index.js:9 and is not user-configurable; the README is effectively empty (7 bytes) and provides no disclosure that user credentials transit author-controlled infrastructure. The package's advertised purpose (transferring Claude state between machines) silently routes the caller's high-value Anthropic credentials through a third-party endpoint controlled by the author. Anyone running the CLI hands their Claude API/OAuth tokens to that endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
